label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out the answer to this question based on the passage. The question is asking what the authorities do when looking for a missing child, and the options are A to D. First, I'll read through the passage again to make sure I understand it. The passage describes the steps taken when a child is missing. They start with an APB (which I know is an "All-Points Bulletin") sent to all police stations. Then they call hospitals because the child might have been in an accident. Next, they try contacting parents, friends, or relatives. They follow the child's movements and check local or national papers the child might read. Other methods include putting up posters, appearing on TV, and using a magazine in America with photos of missing children. The passage also mentions that with nearly two million missing children each year, they can't do all these things for every case because they lack time, money, and personnel. Now, the question is asking which of the options is correct according to the passage. Let's look at the options: A) can always find him B) couldn't always try every way mentioned above C) just try one way or another D) won't give up until they find him Option A says they can always find the child. But the passage doesn't say that. In fact, it implies that with so many missing children, they can't do everything for everyone, so it's possible they don't always find them. So A is probably incorrect. Option B says they couldn't always try every way mentioned above. The passage says, "we can't do all these things for everyone. We haven't got the time, or the money, or the people who work for it." So this matches B's statement that they couldn't always try every method. This seems correct. Option C says they just try one way or another. But the passage lists multiple steps they take (APB, hospitals, parents, papers, posters, TV, magazine), so it's not just one way. So C is probably incorrect. Option D says they won't give up until they find him. The passage doesn't explicitly say they never give
